title: sorbus malus
intro: Sorbus malus, also known as the rowan or mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and bright red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, with applications such as treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. The rowan has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-arthritic effects, which can help protect against oxidative stress and joint inflammation. Herbal preparations of Sorbus malus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
